title: Carbohydrate Supplementation for Elite Long-Distance Endurance — Paper Catalogue
summary: Carbohydrate Supplementation for Elite Long-Distance Endurance — Paper Catalogue Ingested: 2026-05-04 Source: raw/nutrients-17-00918.pdf Synthesised into: wiki/nutrition.md --- ## Paper — Cao et al. 2025 Title: A Review of Carbohydrate Supplementation Approaches and Strategies for Optimizing Performance in Elite Long-Distance Endurance Authors: Wei Cao, Yong He, Ronghua Fu, Yiru Chen, Jiabei Yu, Zihong He Journal: Nutrients Year: 2025 Volume/Issue: 17, 918 DOI: 10.3390/nu17050918 PMID: (open access — MDPI) Funding: General Administration of Sport of China (race walking & marathon focus) Type: Narrative Review --- ## Scientific Quality Assessment | Criterion | Rating | Notes | |---|---|---| | Journal | Moderate | Nutrients (MDPI, IF ~5) — reputable, open access, peer-reviewed | | Evidence type | Low-Moderate | Narrative review, not systematic; no PRISMA, no risk-of-bias | | Primary sources cited | Good | Cites Jensen, Hargreaves, Spriet, Jeukendrup, IAAF 2019 | | Funding bias risk | Moderate | Chinese sport authority funding; race walking–centric examples | | Novelty | Moderate | Primarily synthesis; environmental and anxiety sections are additive vs. existing literature | Verdict: Worth referencing for environmental modifiers (heat, cold, altitude) and the pre-race anxiety mechanism — not for foundational carbohydrate dosing (better sources exist there).
